name: Helioseismology constraints on the solar interior
requirement: Any candidate framework that modifies fundamental physics inside dense plasma (modified gravity, varying constants, screened scalar fields) must respect helioseismic measurements of the solar sound-speed profile c(r), agreeing with the Standard Solar Model at the 0.1-1% level over r/R_sun = 0.1 - 0.95. The Sun is the most precisely characterized stellar interior available.

regime.description: Solar-interior plasma at temperatures 10^7 K (core) to 10^4 K (surface); densities up to 150 g/cm^3.

current_bound.value: Delta c^2 / c^2 < ~0.1% over most of the solar interior
current_bound.experiment: GONG and SOHO/MDI/HMI acoustic-mode frequency measurements
current_bound.reference: Christensen-Dalsgaard, 'Solar structure and evolution', Living Rev. Solar Phys. 18, 2 (2021); Bahcall, Serenelli, Basu, ApJ 621, L85 (2005), arXiv:astro-ph/0412440

what_it_excludes: Constrains screened-scalar (chameleon, symmetron) modified-gravity proposals at solar densities. Constrains varying-constant scenarios in dense plasma. Constrains DM-energy-loss mechanisms inside the Sun (light scalars, axions) through their effect on the temperature profile.

parameterization.name: Standard Solar Model with framework-dependent modifications. Comparison done in inverted sound-speed and density profiles.
parameterization.reference: Bahcall, Pinsonneault, RMP 67, 781 (1995); Vinyoles et al., ApJ 835, 202 (2017), arXiv:1611.09867 (modern SSM).
parameterization.summary: Stellar-evolution code (GARSTEC, Cesam, MESA) integrates the framework's gravitational and microphysics inputs to produce c(r), rho(r); compared to inverted helioseismic data.

procedural.predicted_observable: Sound-speed profile c(r), density profile rho(r), surface helium abundance Y_s, base of convective zone R_bcz.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Fractional deviation in c(r) constrained at <0.1% over most of the radius.
framework_inputs: Modified gravity (e.g. chameleon screening level inside the sun) or varying constants relevant to nuclear reaction rates and opacities.

primary_references.citation: Christensen-Dalsgaard, 'Solar structure and evolution', Living Rev. Solar Phys. 18, 2 (2021)
Vinyoles et al., 'A New Generation of Standard Solar Models', ApJ 835, 202 (2017)
